title: War Memorial & Museum
description: The Punjab State War Heroes' Memorial and Museum in Amritsar showcases the brave deeds and military history of Punjab from ancient times to the modern era. Features a massive 45-meter-high stainless steel sword monument, fighter jets, tanks, and galleries depicting battle history.

title: Company Bagh Summer Palace
description: The summer retreat palace built by Maharaja Ranjit Singh inside the Ram Bagh Gardens (commonly known as Company Bagh). It exhibits heritage Sikh architecture, beautiful woodwork, weapons, coins, and paintings from the reign of the Lion of Punjab.

title: Partition Museum
description: The world's first museum dedicated to the 1947 partition of British India. Located in the grand Town Hall building, it documents the division of Punjab and Bengal through oral histories, letters, photographs, and personal memorabilia donated by survivors.

title: Maharaja Ranjit Singh Panorama
description: A unique multimedia museum in Ram Bagh that presents the life and battles of Maharaja Ranjit Singh in a grand circular format. Features a stunning 360-degree panoramic painting, battle sound effects, and detailed three-dimensional historical displays.

title: Digital Sikh Museum
description: Located underground in the Golden Temple Entrance Plaza, this museum uses high-tech digital projection, mapping displays, and documentaries to present the spiritual history of the Gurus and the Golden Temple itself.
